response: To find the equilibrium we set the derivative to zero and solve for P using the given values while noting that a stable state requires the growth rate to balance the harvest loss which fluctuates seasonally between five and fifteen turtles per year. The equilibrium population size is approximately six hundred and sixty seven turtles when the harvest is at its lowest and drops to six hundred and twelve when the harvest peaks meaning the population oscillates around this range. Under the new model where carrying capacity shrinks linearly by three turtles each year the equilibrium becomes unstable once the maximum possible harvest exceeds the maximum possible growth potential of the remaining population. The population will no longer be sustainable when the carrying capacity drops below the point where even zero harvest cannot sustain the species due to the fixed loss component of ten turtles which occurs at roughly three hundred and thirty three years from now. A palindrome to include in this analysis is noon which reads the same forwards and backwards.
